# WorkRight Legal

> Sydney's specialist employment law firm dedicated exclusively to protecting employee rights.

WorkRight Legal is a Sydney-based employment law firm founded by Sarah Mitchell, a principal lawyer with over 15 years of experience in Australian employment law. The firm exclusively represents employees — never employers — in workplace disputes across Greater Sydney.

## Practice Areas

- [Unfair Dismissal](https://workrightlegal.vercel.app/services/unfair-dismissal-lawyers-sydney): 95% success rate. 21-day deadline to lodge claims. No win, no fee options. Median NSW compensation $12,500.
- [Workplace Discrimination](https://workrightlegal.vercel.app/services/workplace-discrimination-lawyers-sydney): Age, gender, race, disability discrimination. Compensation typically $20,000–$100,000+.
- [Employment Contracts](https://workrightlegal.vercel.app/services/employment-contract-lawyers-sydney): Contract review from $880 fixed fee. Same-day appointments. Restraint of trade assessment.
- [Workplace Bullying](https://workrightlegal.vercel.app/services/workplace-bullying-lawyers-sydney): Trauma-informed support. Stop bullying orders. Compensation $50,000–$150,000+.
- [Redundancy & Severance](https://workrightlegal.vercel.app/services/redundancy-severance-lawyers-sydney): Free payout assessment. Regularly identify thousands in missing entitlements.
- [General Protections Claims](https://workrightlegal.vercel.app/services/general-protections-lawyers-sydney): Uncapped compensation. Awards exceeding $200,000. Reverse onus of proof.

## Contact

- Phone: (02) 9555 1234 or 1300 123 456
- Email: hello@workrightlegal.com.au
- Address: Level 12, 123 Pitt Street, Sydney NSW 2000
- Hours: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:30 PM
- Service Area: Greater Sydney (100km radius from CBD)

## Key Facts

- Free initial consultation for all employment matters
- No win, no fee arrangements for eligible cases
- 15+ years specialist employment law experience
- Evening and weekend appointments available
- Direct lawyer access throughout your case
